prerequisites: A Sentry account with access to the target organization and project. The organization slug is supplied via the required `organization` field and the project slug via the required `project` field.

Permission to create a Sentry auth token. Organization auth tokens can be created by any user for organizations they belong to, and an internal integration can be created to obtain a token with customizable scopes; personal tokens can be created by any user for the organizations and projects they can access.

A Sentry auth token supplied via the required `auth_token` field, with scopes covering the data you want to read. Each Sentry API endpoint documentation page lists the required permissions in its 'Scopes' section.
For self-hosted Sentry, the base hostname of your instance, supplied via the optional `hostname` field. When omitted, the connector targets Sentry's hosted service at sentry.io.

authMethods.name: Auth token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single Sentry auth token, provided via the `auth_token` secret field and sent in the HTTP `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Sentry accepts organization auth tokens, internal integration tokens, and personal (user) auth tokens in this header. Organization and internal-integration tokens are bound to an organization and are not tied to a specific user; personal tokens inherit the access of the user who created them.

setupSteps.title: Choose a token type and create it in Sentry
setupSteps.body: Decide between an organization-bound token or a personal token. For an organization auth token, go to Settings > Developer Settings > Organization Tokens and create a token. For broader, customizable permissions, create an internal integration: open Settings > Developer Settings, create a new internal integration, name it, and select the scopes your reads require. For a personal token, open the account dropdown and go to the Personal Tokens page. Copy the generated token; organization tokens are shown only once, right after creation.

setupSteps.title: Provide the auth token
setupSteps.body: Paste the token into the required `auth_token` field. The connector sends it as `Authorization: Bearer <auth_token>` on every Sentry API request. Grant scopes that cover the resources you sync (for example read access to the organization, projects, issues, and events); each Sentry API endpoint documentation page lists the required scopes in its 'Scopes' section.

setupSteps.title: Set the organization and project
setupSteps.body: Enter your Sentry organization slug in the required `organization` field and the project slug in the required `project` field. These identify which organization and project the connector reads from.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional settings
setupSteps.body: For self-hosted Sentry, set the optional `hostname` field to your instance's host so requests target it instead of sentry.io. Use the optional `discover_fields` setting to control fields returned by Discover-based streams, and `num_workers` to tune request concurrency. Leave these unset to use defaults.

sources.title: Tutorial: Create a Sentry Authentication Token - Sentry Docs
